title: phone_focus_mode CPU/overheat hardening
objective: Eliminate redundant per-tick firewall and rule rebuilds in the on-device focus enforcer that were pegging netd and overheating the phone, without weakening enforcement or self-healing, and whitelist the wake_alarm_sync companion app.
acceptance_criteria: DNS chain is rebuilt only when missing, detached from OUTPUT, or the wrong rule count; identical rebuilds are skipped.
Curfew net reassert runs every 15s instead of every 1s.
Blocked-system-apps file is built once at startup; is_allowed() is memoized per tick.
com.kuhy.wake_alarm_sync is present in both the general and curfew-night whitelists.
shellcheck --severity=warning passes on all four scripts and the no-polling-antipatterns hook passes.
out_of_scope: Replacing the interim iptables stopgap with a netd/eBPF per-UID firewall.
Any change to the Linux-side focus daemon (this is the Android/ADB enforcer).
verifier: shellcheck --severity=warning + pre-commit no-polling-antipatterns hook + on-device netd CPU observation
